title: Bring back automation receipt that included a time delay
excerpt: Last year there was one this year and it is no longer available. ex status changes wait x minutes days then notify or archive etc

posts.author: ChristineWLF (Christine)
posts.cookedHtml: Last year there was one this year and it is no longer available. ex status changes wait x minutes days then notify or archive etc

posts.author: martin340 (Martrin)
posts.cookedHtml:
[quote=“Christine, post:1, topic:103844, full:true, username:ChristineWLF”]
Last year there was one this year and it is no longer available. ex status changes wait x minutes days then notify or archive etc
[/quote] Puntaje Sisben

Hello,
It sounds like you’re referring to an automation or rule in a system like a ticketing tool, CRM, or similar platform, where status changes triggered specific actions (e.g., waiting a certain period, sending notifications, or archiving).

Could you clarify:

What platform or tool you’re using (e.g., Zendesk, Salesforce, Jira)?
What specific feature or automation you’re trying to replace or recreate?
Best Regards
martin

posts.author: ChristineWLF (Christine)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to archive the line but it’s connected to another board so I need a delay to ensure the “when status changes change another status” automation has time to register the status change to connected board before line archives/vanishes and other board may or may not receive the update.
